headline: 2D Ising quenched from T=∞ to T=1.498 (0.66·T_c), L=512, 48 seeds: domain length grows as a clean power law L(t)∼t^n with n=0.486±0.001 (stat, R²=0.9999) from the correlation length; energy-length cross-check n=0.469, drifting to n≈0.496 on the late window. The effective exponent sits a few percent below the asymptotic Allen–Cahn ½ — the documented preasymptotic correction — so the honest systematic band is ≈±0.02. Glauber coarsening exponent consistent with Allen–Cahn t^(1/2) · 34s
